commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: And he said, &c. "According to the measure of each one"s diligence," says Euthymius, "are honour and reward measured out to him."

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
All the disciples of Christ have not the same degree of honour in this world, nor in the next; because all do not make an equal use of the graces they receive. Some are in the first rank, as apostles; then those, to whom the gift of prophecy has been committed; then doctors each exalted according to his merit. For there are many mansions, and many degrees of glory, in the house of the heavenly Father. (Calmet)
For there is one brightness of the sun, another of the moon, and another of the stars; for star differeth from star in brightness. (1 Corinthians xv. 41.)

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
